courseName: Turbine Aeroplane Aerodynamics Structures and Systems 4
deptName: ช่างอากาศยาน
standardRef: EASA PART 66 MODULE 11A TURBINE AEROPLANE AERODYNAMICS STRUCTURES AND SYSTEMS
learningOutcomes:
Be able to know, understand, explain, apply and codify knowledge for the Electrical
Power, Onboard Maintenance system, Integrated Modular Avionic, Cabin System and
Information System in practical and have a good attitude to work neatly, be patience with
work, a habit of working carefully and safety.
objectives:
1. Be able to know the Electrical Power, Aircraft Instruments, Onboard Maintenance
system, Integrated Modular Avionic, Cabin System and Information System.
2. Be able to understand and explain the Electrical Power, Onboard Maintenance
system, Integrated Modular Avionic, Cabin System and Information System.
3. Have a good attitude to work neatly, be patience with work, a habit of working
carefully and safety.
4. Be able to apply aircraft the Electrical Power, Onboard Maintenance system, Integrated
Modular Avionic, Cabin System and Information System in practical and higher education.
competencies:
1. Describe and codify knowledge about the Electrical Power, Onboard Maintenance
system, Integrated Modular Avionic, Cabin System and Information System.
2. Apply knowledge for Electrical Power, Onboard Maintenance system, Integrated
Modular Avionic, Cabin System and Information System in higher education.
description:
To study about Electrical Power (ATA24), Integrated Modular Avionic ATA42, Cabin
System ATA44, Onboard Maintenance system ATA 45including Central maintenance
computers, Data loading system, electronic library system, Printing Structure monitoring
(damage tolerance monitoring and Information System ATA 46)
